source: On The Tape / RiskReversal — Fear is the market killer
summary: Semiconductors remain an exciting area for venture investing; Baker has 22 years of comfort investing in semis
summary_extended: Baker expresses a broad, long-standing enthusiasm for semiconductors as an investment category, noting he has 22 years of experience investing in the sector. He highlights that there are particularly exciting developments happening at the venture stage in semiconductors, suggesting early-stage innovation is accelerating. Baker frames semiconductors alongside internet, software, application infrastructure, and direct-to-consumer as the areas where he seeks the highest risk-adjusted internal rate of return. However, he does not name any specific companies, sub-sectors, or catalysts, making this a general thematic preference rather than an actionable single-stock thesis. No specific numbers, timeframes, or comparative valuations are cited.
detail: Baker mentions semiconductors as an area he loves investing in, with a lot of exciting things happening at the venture stage. He frames it alongside internet, software, application infrastructure, and D2C as areas where he seeks highest risk-adjusted IRR.
confidence_evidence: Baker says 'love investing in semiconductors, there's a lot of exciting thing happening at semis in venture' — positive but brief mention without elaboration.
quote_fragment: love investing in semiconductors, there's a lot of exciting thing happening at semis in venture
